steps.description: Click the search bar under the "Samples" section header in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface to start entering a search query.
Type the text 'to do list spfx' into the search input field located in the "Samples" pane of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Create" button for the "Todo List with SPFX" sample project in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select a project root location.
@assertion 'TODO-LIST-SPFX' is exist
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, specifically within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension interface.
Type text: 'provision' into the command palette search input field at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit context.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the provisioning command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, where "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" is selected.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code interface, specifically within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension workspace.

Type text into Visual Studio Code's Command Palette: 'Notifications: Show Notifications', interacting with a dropdown menu displaying matching commands such as "Notifications: Accept Notification Primary Action" and others.
Press "Enter" to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion notification "provision stage executed successfully" exist.
Press key: F1 to activate the help or command palette in Visual Studio Code, displayed within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace.
Type text: 'Terminal:Create New Terminal' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to execute the "Create New Terminal" command from the dropdown list.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the selected command "Terminal: Create New Terminal" in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.
Type text in the Terminal input field within Visual Studio Code: 'google-chrome https://teams.microsoft.com/v2'.
Press the "Enter" key in the integrated terminal within Visual Studio Code's Teams Toolkit project folder to execute the command.
Click the "Email, phone, or Skype" text field in the Microsoft login interface to focus the input for entering credentials.
Type text into the Microsoft login form email field with the value: '${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}} '
Press the "Enter" key to confirm input for the email address field after typing '${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}} ' on the Microsoft login page.
Click on the "Password" field in the Microsoft login interface to activate the text input for entering the password.

Type text:  ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_PASSWORD}}  into the password input field on the Microsoft login page, below the "Enter password" label, for the account '${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}'.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password on the Microsoft login page by triggering the "Sign in" button.
Click the "Yes" button in the "Stay signed in?" prompt on the Microsoft login screen.
